original_description: Compound Component pattern for composable, flexible React UI. Context + dot notation. Use when: building UI with shared implicit state, flexible slot-based APIs, design system components. Trigger on: "compound component", "componente composable", "context UI", "dot notation component", "Card.Header", "flexible component API", "Radix-style", "Headless UI pattern".
best_description: Compound Component pattern for composable, flexible React UI. Context + dot notation. Use when: building UI with shared implicit state, flexible slot-based APIs, design system components. Trigger on: "compound component", "componente composable", "context UI", "dot notation component", "Card.Header", "flexible component API", "Radix-style", "Headless UI pattern".
final_description: Invoke when someone needs to build a React component with dot-notation sub-parts (Modal.Header, Tabs.Panel, Select.Trigger, Stepper.Step) that implicitly share state — the Compound Component pattern. Prime signals: designing sub-components that communicate without prop drilling, building composable widget APIs like Radix UI or shadcn/ui, structuring a complex UI widget where multiple independent pieces share a Context. Also use for: TypeScript dot-notation component APIs, slot-based UI components where consumers can reorder or omit parts, or any "how do I structure X with sub-components?" question about Accordion, Select, Modal, Tabs, Table, or Stepper.

history.description: Use this skill when building React components whose sub-parts need to share implicit state without prop drilling — e.g., a Modal where Header, Body, and Footer are independent sub-components, a Tabs where parent controls active state, a Select where Trigger and Options communicate silently. This is the Compound Component pattern: Context + dot notation (Component.SubPart). Reach for it when: designing a flexible slot-based component API, letting consumers compose and reorder sub-parts freely, or building in the style of Radix UI / Headless UI. Skip it for simple components with no shared state between parts or fixed single-structure layouts.
